rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/25. Examiner unavailable. Issues necessitating appearance: 1) A Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit has not been filed yet by David Long, the purported successor-in-interest to Nancy Bass. 2) Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters verifying the ending balance(s). [LRSF 14.34 D.] 3) It is unclear why the carry value of the Cousins Properties Inc. shares changed so significantly from the value shown at the end of the last accounting. No explanation was given. 4) What does the personal representative propose to do about the funds still held in Mr. O'Neil's client trust account? If she is seeking relief, it is not in the prayer. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Interest on pecuniary bequests is set forth in the Probate Code, not pursuant to an examiner. Only financial institution statements verifying the ending balances are required to be filed.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/20/2024 - see also tentative rulings for 11/26/2024 & 12/31/2024 hearings): 1) No GC-020 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on the conservatee at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Allegations required by Probate Code section 1064(a)(1)-(4) must be made. See also CRC 7.575(a)(3). 3) An allegation must be made re sufficiency of bond. See LRSF 14.34(E). 4) The summary of account must be filed on the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-400(SUM). See CRC 7.575(e)(5). 5) The total amount of non-cash assets on hand at beginning of account period ($2,856,572.55) does not match the total listed on the final inventory and appraisal ($2,846,571.95). It should be noted that the final inventory and appraisal (and also the petition) listed 06/02/2022 as the date of appointment, but the beginning date of the account is 06/06/2022. It should also be noted that the corrected partial inventory and appraisal listed 06/06/2022 as the date of appointment. 6) No supplemental inventory and appraisal has been filed and served for the UPS and OMC shares reported as additional property received (Schedule 1C). See section 2613. 7) No schedule of liabilities (see section 1063(g)) was included. The fees and costs of the former court-appointed attorney for the conservatee were approved by the Court on 07/20/2023, but the schedule of disbursements does not show payment. 8) Schedule F does not show purchase of "The Charles Schwab 4%" (corporate bond) listed on Schedule E. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEAN YUKE CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/5/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributeeâs name and description of the property, including the complete legal description, street address (if any), and assessor's parcel number (if any) of real property. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] The legal description of the real property must also be in the order for final distribution. (2) Have all of the heirs consented to the non-pro rata distribution of all of the tangible personal property to Sharon Yuke Chan without any equalization in the cash? (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (4) A proposed order should not have exhibits. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/26/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TESSIE L. HENRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the proof of service filed on 12/16/24 could not be true, as it was verified on 11/18/24, when the date of mailing was incomplete. The corrected proof of service should have been verified after counsel was notified that the proof of service was incomplete, so sometime after 12/13/24. (The "supplement" also amended the statutory fee, when the issue of the statutory fee calculation was just pointed out to the attorney and not the reason for the continuance.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MICHAEL J. AND BARBARA A. RUSSELL 2002 TRUST, dated december 27, 2002 as amended and restated
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Order Approving Settlement Pursuant To Probate Code Section 17200
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/4/25. (No earlier availability.) (1) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Notice was mailed to Michael Nicholson...? Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Notice must not be mailed until after the pleading requiring notice is E-FILED. Since the "amendment" that was e-filed on 11/25/24 actually just supplemented the address of Bryan's attorney, the Court will overlook the titling of that pleading and notice of the "amendment" mailed before the "amendment" was e-filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANA BERTHA ACOSTA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Isreal (or Israel) Cruz, the decedent's surviving spouse and one of the heirs having higher priority for his own appointment under Probate Code section 8461(a) or for appointment of his nominee under section 8465(b), has not filed a declination to serve, waiver of his priority, and/or nomination of either petitioner as administrator of this estate. Section 8463 does not apply in favor of the petitioners as the surviving spouse, though he may have been living apart from the decedent at the time of her death, was no longer a party to the related divorce case (FDI-13-780478) after it was dismissed by the decedent in 2021. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
